title: VimFu — gx — Open URL
description: Use gx to open the URL or file path under the cursor with your system's default application.

setup.comment: Set TERM for proper color support in Neovim

setup.content:
Open a local file:
notes.txt

Web link example:
https://neovim.io

Opened by gx

This is a local file.

setup.line: nvim --cmd 'lua vim.ui.open = function(path) vim.cmd.edit(path) end' urls.txt

steps.say: The g key, then the x key, opens the file path or URL under the cursor with your system's default application.
First, use it on a local file path. Move to `notes.txt`.
Press the g key, then the x key. For this recording, local files open back inside Neovim so you can see it happen.
`notes.txt` opened in this Neovim window. On a normal desktop, the same keys hand the path to the system opener.
The same key works on web links too; in a desktop session, the URL opens in your browser.
The g key, then the x key: one command for opening file paths and URLs.
